full_response:
The trace of the metric tensor is computed using the definition of the trace of a tensor \(S_{ab}\), which is \(T = g^{ab} S_{ab}\). Setting \(S_{ab} = g_{ab}\), the trace becomes \(T = g^{ab} g_{ab}\).  

The inverse metric satisfies \(g^{ab} g_{bc} = \delta^a_c\). Contracting the indices \(a\) and \(c\) gives \(g^{ab} g_{ba} = \delta^a_a\). Since the metric is symmetric, \(g_{ab} = g_{ba}\), so \(g^{ab} g_{ab} = g^{ab} g_{ba} = \delta^a_a\).  

In four spacetime dimensions, \(\delta^a_a = 4\). Therefore, the trace of the metric is 4.

\boxed{4}
Confidence: 100%
